name: Pork Milanese

ingredients.item: pork chops (bone-in or boneless) pounded thin.

method: Prepare3 small bowls, mix one for the beaten egg, one for flour, and one for breadcrumbs, seasonings, and cheese. On a cutting board covered with plastic wrap, pound the pork chops ¼ inch thin using a mallet. If you are working with thick cut pork chops you can cut them in half before pounding them. Drench in flour, then eggs, then breadcrumbs. Set aside. This recipe makes 2-3 pork chops. Double recipe if you need to feed more people or you eat more than one pork chop.

In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil with extra oil ready to add if needed. Fry each side of the pork chop for about two minutes before turning. Cook thoroughly until the inside is white. Transfer to plates and garnish with lemons. Serving suggestion: roasted vegetables, mixed salad, and fresh bread. Serves 2. Olé
